sections.paragraph:
Δίρκη, ης, ἡ Dirce, wife of the Theban king Lycus; she was dragged to death by a wild bull. Pl. (with Δίρκαι=people like Dirce cp. Themist., Or. 285c … Τιμάιους, Δικαιάρχους=men like Timaeus, Dicaearchus; Himerius, Or. [Ecl.] 5, 24 Θησεῖς κ. Κόδροι; schol. on Plato, Tht. 169b; Synesius, Calvit. 21 p. 85b) of Christian women who were martyred 1 Cl 6:2; s. Δαναΐδες (cp. Diod S 14, 112, 1 τραγικήν τινα τιμωρίαν λαμβάνειν=receive a punishment of the kind found in tragedy). Kl. Pauly II 99.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Themist Themist , IV a.d.—List 5

Timaeus Timaeus , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Dicaearchus Dicaearchus , IV b.c.—List 5

Himerius Himerius , IV a.d.—List 5

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Kl. Pauly Kl. Pauly = Der Kleine Pauly: Lexikon der Antike—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 252
